trans-unit.source: Exercise 4: Visualizing Cyclone Impact Results – Aina Styles Her Layers
trans-unit.target: Exercise 4: Visualizing Cyclone Impact Results – Aina Styles Her Layers

trans-unit.source: Characteristics of the exercise
trans-unit.target: Characteristics of the exercise

trans-unit.source: ::::{grid} 2 :::{grid-item-card} __Type of trainings exercise:__ ^^^
trans-unit.target: ::::{grid} 2 :::{grid-item-card} __Type of trainings exercise:__ ^^^

trans-unit.source: This exercise can be used in online and presence training.
trans-unit.target: This exercise can be used in online and presence training.

trans-unit.source: It can be done as a follow-along exercise or individually as a self-study.
trans-unit.target: It can be done as a follow-along exercise or individually as a self-study.

trans-unit.source: :::{grid-item-card} __Exercise Track:__
trans-unit.target: :::{grid-item-card} __Exercise Track:__

trans-unit.source: This exercise is part of the [Madagascar Anticipatory Action Cyclon Analysis Exercise Track](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Exercise_tracks/en_mdg_aa_cyclones.html).
trans-unit.target: This exercise is part of the [Madagascar Anticipatory Action Cyclon Analysis Exercise Track](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Exercise_tracks/en_mdg_aa_cyclones.html).

trans-unit.source: ::::{grid} 2 :::{grid-item-card} __Estimated time demand for the exercise__ ^^^
trans-unit.target: ::::{grid} 2 :::{grid-item-card} __Estimated time demand for the exercise__ ^^^

trans-unit.source: [Zonal Statistics](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_raster_basic_wiki.html)
trans-unit.target: [Zonal Statistics](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_raster_basic_wiki.html)

trans-unit.source: [Intersection](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_spatial_joins_wiki.html#join-attributes-by-location-summary)
trans-unit.target: [Intersection](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_spatial_joins_wiki.html#join-attributes-by-location-summary)

trans-unit.source: [Projections](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_projections_wiki.md)
trans-unit.target: [Projections](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_projections_wiki.md)

trans-unit.source: [Buffer](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_projections_wiki.md)
trans-unit.target: [Buffer](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_projections_wiki.md)

trans-unit.source: [Clip](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_projections_wiki.md)
trans-unit.target: [Clip](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_projections_wiki.md)

trans-unit.source: [Automatisation](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_automatisation_wiki.html)
trans-unit.target: [Automatisation](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_automatisation_wiki.html)

trans-unit.source: :::{card} :class-card: sd-border-1 sd-shadow-none __Aim of the exercise:__ ^^^ Aina, the GIS expert at the Malagasy Red Cross (CRM), is preparing for the upcoming cyclone season. She wants to improve her team’s ability to act quickly once a storm is forecasted by automating key analyses in QGIS. These include estimating exposed populations, identifying impacted services like health and education, and assessing whether health posts can be reached from key warehouses within a critical 10-hour window. The goal is to prepare an end-to-end analysis and visualization workflow that can support fast, data-driven anticipatory action before a cyclone makes landfall.
trans-unit.target: :::{card} :class-card: sd-border-1 sd-shadow-none __Aim of the exercise:__ ^^^ Aina, the GIS expert at the Malagasy Red Cross (CRM), is preparing for the upcoming cyclone season. She wants to improve her team’s ability to act quickly once a storm is forecasted by automating key analyses in QGIS. These include estimating exposed populations, identifying impacted services like health and education, and assessing whether health posts can be reached from key warehouses within a critical 10-hour window. The goal is to prepare an end-to-end analysis and visualization workflow that can support fast, data-driven anticipatory action before a cyclone makes landfall.

trans-unit.source: Instructions for the trainers
trans-unit.target: Instructions for the trainers

trans-unit.source: :::{dropdown} __Trainers Corner__
trans-unit.target: :::{dropdown} __Trainers Corner__

trans-unit.source: Prepare the training
trans-unit.target: Prepare the training

trans-unit.source: Take the time to familiarise yourself with the exercise and the provided material.
trans-unit.target: Take the time to familiarise yourself with the exercise and the provided material.

trans-unit.source: Prepare a white-board. It can be either a physical whiteboard, a flip-chart, or a digital whiteboard (e.g. Miro board) where the participants can add their findings and questions.
trans-unit.target: Prepare a white-board. It can be either a physical whiteboard, a flip-chart, or a digital whiteboard (e.g. Miro board) where the participants can add their findings and questions.

trans-unit.source: Before starting the exercise, make sure everybody has installed QGIS and has downloaded __and unzipped__ the data folder.
trans-unit.target: Before starting the exercise, make sure everybody has installed QGIS and has downloaded __and unzipped__ the data folder.

trans-unit.source: Check out [How to do trainings?](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Trainers_corner/en_how_to_training.html#how-to-do-trainings) for some general tips on conducting trainings.
trans-unit.target: Check out [How to do trainings?](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Trainers_corner/en_how_to_training.html#how-to-do-trainings) for some general tips on conducting trainings.

trans-unit.source: Conduct the training
trans-unit.target: Conduct the training

trans-unit.source: __Introduction:__
trans-unit.target: __Introduction:__

trans-unit.source: Introduce the idea and aim of the exercise.
trans-unit.target: Introduce the idea and aim of the exercise.

trans-unit.source: Provide the download link and make sure everybody has unzipped the folder before beginning the tasks.
trans-unit.target: Provide the download link and make sure everybody has unzipped the folder before beginning the tasks.

trans-unit.source: __Follow-along:__
trans-unit.target: __Follow-along:__

trans-unit.source: Show and explain each step yourself at least twice and slow enough so everybody can see what you are doing, and follow along in their own QGIS-project.
trans-unit.target: Show and explain each step yourself at least twice and slow enough so everybody can see what you are doing, and follow along in their own QGIS-project.

trans-unit.source: Make sure that everybody is following along and doing the steps themselves by periodically asking if anybody needs help or if everybody is still following.
trans-unit.target: Make sure that everybody is following along and doing the steps themselves by periodically asking if anybody needs help or if everybody is still following.

trans-unit.source: Be open and patient to every question or problem that might come up. Your participants are essentially multitasking by paying attention to your instructions and orienting themselves in their own QGIS-project.
trans-unit.target: Be open and patient to every question or problem that might come up. Your participants are essentially multitasking by paying attention to your instructions and orienting themselves in their own QGIS-project.

trans-unit.source: __Wrap up:__
trans-unit.target: __Wrap up:__

trans-unit.source: Leave time for any issues or questions concerning the tasks at the end of the exercise.
trans-unit.target: Leave time for any issues or questions concerning the tasks at the end of the exercise.

trans-unit.source: Leave some time for open questions.
trans-unit.target: Leave some time for open questions.

trans-unit.source: Available data
trans-unit.target: Available data

trans-unit.source: __Download all datasets here, save the folder on your computer and unzip the file.__ :::
trans-unit.target: __Download all datasets here, save the folder on your computer and unzip the file.__ :::

trans-unit.source: Context
trans-unit.target: Context

trans-unit.source: Aina now has all the analysis results she needs — but numbers and tables alone won’t convince her colleagues or decision-makers. What they need are clear and easy-to-read maps that can be used directly in meetings and reports.
trans-unit.target: Aina now has all the analysis results she needs — but numbers and tables alone won’t convince her colleagues or decision-makers. What they need are clear and easy-to-read maps that can be used directly in meetings and reports.

trans-unit.source: To save time, Aina doesn’t want to adjust colors and legends manually each time a new cyclone comes in. Instead, she will use ready-made style files (.qml) that instantly give layers a professional and consistent look. Where no style exists yet, she will create one herself, so that next time the map can be updated with just a few clicks.
trans-unit.target: To save time, Aina doesn’t want to adjust colors and legends manually each time a new cyclone comes in. Instead, she will use ready-made style files (.qml) that instantly give layers a professional and consistent look. Where no style exists yet, she will create one herself, so that next time the map can be updated with just a few clicks.

trans-unit.source: In this task, you will help Aina make her cyclone impact maps both informative and visually compelling by applying and creating QGIS style files.
trans-unit.target: In this task, you will help Aina make her cyclone impact maps both informative and visually compelling by applying and creating QGIS style files.

trans-unit.source: Tasks
trans-unit.target: Tasks

trans-unit.source: 1. **Load Required Layers (if not already loaded)**
trans-unit.target: 1. **Load Required Layers (if not already loaded)**

trans-unit.source: Make sure the following layers are already loaded into your QGIS project. These are outputs from **Task 3**:
trans-unit.target: Make sure the following layers are already loaded into your QGIS project. These are outputs from **Task 3**:

trans-unit.source: If any are missing:
trans-unit.target: If any are missing:

trans-unit.source: Load them using **drag & drop** from your `results` folder, or
trans-unit.target: Load them using **drag & drop** from your `results` folder, or

trans-unit.source: Use `Layer` → `Add Layer` → `Add Vector Layer` or `Add Raster Layer`.
trans-unit.target: Use `Layer` → `Add Layer` → `Add Vector Layer` or `Add Raster Layer`.

trans-unit.source: 2. **Apply Predefined Style Files**
trans-unit.target: 2. **Apply Predefined Style Files**

trans-unit.source: Apply the following`.qml` style files to the respective layers:
trans-unit.target: Apply the following`.qml` style files to the respective layers:

trans-unit.source: **Layer**
trans-unit.target: **Layer**

trans-unit.source: **Style File**
trans-unit.target: **Style File**

trans-unit.source: :::{note} ⚠️ For the **health** and **education facilities**, the provided style files are linked to the column containing the **sum of exposed facilities**. They are **not** based on the percentage column. :::
trans-unit.target: :::{note} ⚠️ For the **health** and **education facilities**, the provided style files are linked to the column containing the **sum of exposed facilities**. They are **not** based on the percentage column. :::

trans-unit.source: **Steps:**
trans-unit.target: **Steps:**

trans-unit.source: Right-click on the layer in the **Layers Panel**.
trans-unit.target: Right-click on the layer in the **Layers Panel**.

trans-unit.source: Select `Properties`.
trans-unit.target: Select `Properties`.

trans-unit.source: In the window that opens, go to the `Symbology` tab.
trans-unit.target: In the window that opens, go to the `Symbology` tab.

trans-unit.source: At the bottom left, click `Style` → `Load Style…`.
trans-unit.target: At the bottom left, click `Style` → `Load Style…`.

trans-unit.source: Click on the three points ![](/fig/Three_points.png).
trans-unit.target: Click on the three points ![](/fig/Three_points.png).

trans-unit.source: Navigate to the corresponding `.qml` file in the folder `layer_sytle` and select it.
trans-unit.target: Navigate to the corresponding `.qml` file in the folder `layer_sytle` and select it.

trans-unit.source: Click `Open`, then `Apply` and `OK` to confirm.
trans-unit.target: Click `Open`, then `Apply` and `OK` to confirm.

trans-unit.source: 💡 *If the style doesn’t load correctly, double-check the column names and make sure the column name used in the `.qml` file matches the one in your layer. To do this, open the **Attribute Table** of the layer and compare field names.*
trans-unit.target: 💡 *If the style doesn’t load correctly, double-check the column names and make sure the column name used in the `.qml` file matches the one in your layer. To do this, open the **Attribute Table** of the layer and compare field names.*

trans-unit.source: ::::{tab-item} Intermediate result: Exposed Population
trans-unit.target: ::::{tab-item} Intermediate result: Exposed Population

trans-unit.source: Map showing the numberof persons exposed per district using the .qml style file. ::: :::: ::::{tab-item} Intermediate result: Exposed Health Facilities :::{figure} /fig/fr_MDG_AA_intermediate_result_model_task4_exposed_HS_sum_style.PNG
trans-unit.target: Map showing the numberof persons exposed per district using the .qml style file. ::: :::: ::::{tab-item} Intermediate result: Exposed Health Facilities :::{figure} /fig/fr_MDG_AA_intermediate_result_model_task4_exposed_HS_sum_style.PNG

trans-unit.source: Map showing the exposed health establishments per district. ::: :::: ::::{tab-item} Intermediate result: Exposed Education Facilities :::{figure} /fig/fr_MDG_AA_intermediate_result_model_task4_exposed_ES_sum_style.PNG
trans-unit.target: Map showing the exposed health establishments per district. ::: :::: ::::{tab-item} Intermediate result: Exposed Education Facilities :::{figure} /fig/fr_MDG_AA_intermediate_result_model_task4_exposed_ES_sum_style.PNG

trans-unit.source: Map showing the number of education institutions exposed per district. ::: :::: :::::
trans-unit.target: Map showing the number of education institutions exposed per district. ::: :::: :::::

trans-unit.source: 3. **Style Percentage Layers Manually**
trans-unit.target: 3. **Style Percentage Layers Manually**

trans-unit.source: Aina also wants to visualise the percentage of exposed health and education facilities. However, since there is no prepared style available, she must complete the process manually.
trans-unit.target: Aina also wants to visualise the percentage of exposed health and education facilities. However, since there is no prepared style available, she must complete the process manually.

trans-unit.source: **Steps:**
trans-unit.target: **Steps:**

trans-unit.source: <kbd>Right-click</kbd> on the layer `admin2_health_affected` → select **Duplicate Layer**
trans-unit.target: <kbd>Right-click</kbd> on the layer `admin2_health_affected` → select **Duplicate Layer**

trans-unit.source: **Rename** the duplicated layer to:
trans-unit.target: **Rename** the duplicated layer to:

trans-unit.source: Right-click on the layer in the **Layers Panel**
trans-unit.target: Right-click on the layer in the **Layers Panel**

trans-unit.source: Select **Properties**
trans-unit.target: Select **Properties**

trans-unit.source: In the window that opens, go to the **Symbology** tab
trans-unit.target: In the window that opens, go to the **Symbology** tab

trans-unit.source: Set **Symbology** to `Graduated`
trans-unit.target: Set **Symbology** to `Graduated`

trans-unit.source: Choose the correct **field**:
trans-unit.target: Choose the correct **field**:

trans-unit.source: Open the **Histogram** tab to view the value distribution by clicking on `calculate histogram`
trans-unit.target: Open the **Histogram** tab to view the value distribution by clicking on `calculate histogram`

trans-unit.source: Next go back to `Classes` and set the following configuration:
trans-unit.target: Next go back to `Classes` and set the following configuration:

trans-unit.source: **Mode**: `Equal Interval`
trans-unit.target: **Mode**: `Equal Interval`

trans-unit.source: **Classes**: `4`
trans-unit.target: **Classes**: `4`

trans-unit.source: Click `OK`.This will create four classes (`0–25%`, `25–50%`, `50–75%`, `75–100%`)
trans-unit.target: Click `OK`.This will create four classes (`0–25%`, `25–50%`, `50–75%`, `75–100%`)

trans-unit.source: Choose a color ramp (e.g., light yellow → dark red)
trans-unit.target: Choose a color ramp (e.g., light yellow → dark red)

trans-unit.source: Optionally customize class labels for clarity
trans-unit.target: Optionally customize class labels for clarity

trans-unit.source: Click `Apply`
trans-unit.target: Click `Apply`

trans-unit.source: Repeat the same process for the layer `admin2_education_affected`. After duplicating the layer, rename the new one to:
trans-unit.target: Repeat the same process for the layer `admin2_education_affected`. After duplicating the layer, rename the new one to:

trans-unit.source: 🧠 *Why 4 equal classes?* This helps visualize severity across districts using simple and interpretable risk categories. However, you can experiment with **Natural Breaks** if data is unevenly distributed.
trans-unit.target: 🧠 *Why 4 equal classes?* This helps visualize severity across districts using simple and interpretable risk categories. However, you can experiment with **Natural Breaks** if data is unevenly distributed.

trans-unit.source: 4. **Save Your New Styles for Reuse**
trans-unit.target: 4. **Save Your New Styles for Reuse**

trans-unit.source: Save your manually created styles as `.qml` files for future reuse.
trans-unit.target: Save your manually created styles as `.qml` files for future reuse.

trans-unit.source: **Steps:**
trans-unit.target: **Steps:**

trans-unit.source: Right-click on the layer in the **Layers Panel**
trans-unit.target: Right-click on the layer in the **Layers Panel**

trans-unit.source: Select **Properties**
trans-unit.target: Select **Properties**

trans-unit.source: In the window that opens, go to the **Symbology** tab
trans-unit.target: In the window that opens, go to the **Symbology** tab

trans-unit.source: Click on `Style` → `Save Style…`
trans-unit.target: Click on `Style` → `Save Style…`

trans-unit.source: Save the file in the folder `layer_style`
trans-unit.target: Save the file in the folder `layer_style`

trans-unit.source: Use these filenames:
trans-unit.target: Use these filenames:

trans-unit.source: 5. *(Optional)* Import Styles into Your QGIS Library
trans-unit.target: 5. *(Optional)* Import Styles into Your QGIS Library

trans-unit.source: To reuse your styles in any future project:
trans-unit.target: To reuse your styles in any future project:

trans-unit.source: Go to `Settings` → `Style Manager`.
trans-unit.target: Go to `Settings` → `Style Manager`.

trans-unit.source: Click `Import/Export` → `Import Items`.
trans-unit.target: Click `Import/Export` → `Import Items`.

trans-unit.source: Browse to and select your saved `.qml` files.
trans-unit.target: Browse to and select your saved `.qml` files.

trans-unit.source: The styles will now appear as presets in the **Layer Styling Panel**.
trans-unit.target: The styles will now appear as presets in the **Layer Styling Panel**.
